headline: Ranked Resets: UCLA and Michigan Hunt Redemption
subhead: Two top-ten teams face elimination-game pressure after blowout Week 1 defeats.

xFactor.why: UCLA's signal-caller must limit turnovers and sustain drives after Notre Dame's defense forced critical mistakes in the opener.

keyMatchup: Michigan's defensive front seven against UCLA's ability to establish any semblance of run game; containment wins this one.
body: Both programs enter the Victors' house with matching 0-1 records and identical rankings, but the margin of their defeats tells a different story. Michigan absorbed a 23-point spanking from Ohio State while UCLA fell by 17 to Notre Dame. The common thread is offensive inconsistency—neither team moved the ball with conviction when it mattered most, and UCLA in particular struggled with execution in critical moments.

This matchup hinges on whether either offense can manufacture even modest momentum before the margin gets out of reach again. Michigan at home carries a structural advantage in a pressure spot, though the Wolverines will need their defense to stay disciplined against a UCLA team desperate to prove last week was an outlier. If the Bruins can avoid the explosive plays that buried them against Notre Dame, they keep themselves in the game; Michigan simply cannot afford another flatline offensive performance against a ranked opponent.
